title: Commutative Algebra to Representation Theory, Through the Combinatorics of Filtered RSK
speaker: Alex Yong
abstract:
Suppose X is the affine cone of a projective variety. The Hilbert series of the coordinate ring C[X] is the character of an algebraic torus. More generally, one considers a reductive algebraic group G
 acting rationally on X. When X is matrix space Mat(m,n),G=GLm×GLn acts by row and column operations. The relationship between the Hilbert series and the class of C[Mat(m,n)]
 in the representation ring of G is the Cauchy identity; its combinatorial explanation is the Robinson-Schensted-Knuth (RSK) correspondence. We study X in Matm,n where G=GLm×GLn or a Levi subgroup acts, and there is an additional compatibility of Grobner basis theory with Kashiwara’s crystal basis theory. For such ``bicrystalline’’ varieties, we give a common generalization of the Hilbert series, the Cauchy identity, and the Littlewood-Richardson rule. Our work introduces a ``filtered’’ generalization of RSK. Our main application is to determinantal varieties such as Fulton’s matrix Schubert varieties. This is joint work with Abigail Price (UIUC) and Ada Stelzer (UIUC); arXiv:2403.09938.
tags: Workshop on Combinatorics of Enumerative Geometry

proof_stages.label: Cauchy identity as the organizing problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk frames the Cauchy identity as a common object with combinatorial, representation-theoretic, and commutative-algebraic interpretations, motivating bicrystalline ideals as a synthesis of Groebner bases and crystals.

proof_stages.label: Matrix generating functions and RSK
proof_stages.summary: The left side of Cauchy is identified as the generating series of nonnegative integer matrices by row and column weights, while RSK gives a weight-preserving bijection to pairs of semistandard tableaux yielding the Schur-polynomial sum.

proof_stages.label: Representation-theoretic character interpretation
proof_stages.summary: After recalling representations, weights, tori, and Schur modules, the coordinate ring of matrix space is viewed as a GLm x GLn representation whose character decomposition is equivalent to the Cauchy identity.

proof_stages.label: Hilbert series, standard monomials, and Levi generalization
proof_stages.summary: Groebner bases and standard monomial bases identify Hilbert series with torus characters, and the desired generalization replaces matrix space by a quotient coordinate ring while interpolating from torus actions to full GL actions via block Levi subgroups.

proof_stages.label: Motivating varieties and the concrete multiplicity problem
proof_stages.summary: Determinantal, matrix Schubert, double Bruhat, and related orbit-closure examples lead to the central problem of giving positive rules for Schur-product multiplicities in Levi characters of coordinate rings.

proof_stages.label: Bicrystalline ideals via crystal operators
proof_stages.summary: An ideal is defined to be bicrystalline when its standard monomials are closed under row and column bicrystal operators, whose construction is explained by lifting Kashiwara crystal operators through RSK to matrices.

proof_stages.label: Filtered RSK multiplicity theorem
proof_stages.summary: Filtered RSK colors row and column words by Levi blocks and counts standard monomials mapping to highest-weight tableau tuples, yielding the multiplicity formula, the bicrystallinity of matrix Schubert varieties, and a finite test for bicrystallinity.

proof_stages.label: Applications and concluding thesis
proof_stages.summary: Examples from matroid orbit closures, double Bruhat ideals, and nonreduced invariant-theoretic ideals illustrate the theorem, and the talk concludes that crystal structures on standard monomials provide positive combinatorial rules for irreducible multiplicities.
